title: American Scrambled Egg Breakfast Burrito

subs.name: large wheat chapati or phulka — fold and eat the same way for a fully Indian-pantry version
any coloured shimla mirch (bell pepper) available; green is cheapest and works perfectly
skip the cheese to keep it dairy-free; the scrambled egg filling is satisfying on its own
any cooking oil for dairy-free scrambled eggs; ghee adds a desi flavour

ingredients.name: tomato ketchup or hot sauce, for serving

steps.text: Crack the eggs into a bowl, add black pepper and a pinch of salt, and beat well. Set aside.

Heat the oil in a frying pan on medium flame. Add the diced onion and cook for 2–3 minutes until softened. Add the capsicum and green chilli and stir-fry for 2 minutes until slightly softened but still with some crunch.
Add the diced tomato, stir and cook for 1 minute. Push the vegetable mixture to one side of the pan.

Add the butter to the empty side of the pan. Once melted, pour in the beaten eggs. Cook on medium-low, gently folding the eggs from the edges toward the centre every few seconds. Remove from heat while they are still slightly glossy and just set — about 2–3 minutes. Residual heat will finish them.
Fold the scrambled egg into the vegetables gently. Scatter cheese over the top if using and allow it to melt from the residual heat.

Warm each tortilla directly on the tawa for 20–30 seconds per side until pliable and lightly toasted. Spoon a quarter of the egg filling into the centre of each tortilla, add a drizzle of ketchup, fold up the bottom, tuck in the sides and roll firmly. Serve immediately.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: tortilla → large wheat chapati or phulka — fold and eat the same way for a fully Indian-pantry version; cheese → skip the cheese to keep it dairy-free; the scrambled egg filling is satisfying on its own; capsicum → any coloured shimla mirch (bell pepper) available; green is cheapest and works perfectly; butter → any cooking oil for dairy-free scrambled eggs; ghee adds a desi flavour
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Flour tortillas are now sold in most Indian supermarkets and Reliance Fresh outlets. If unavailable, use a large soft wheat chapati — it behaves identically. The egg-vegetable filling is essentially a masala scrambled egg, familiar to any Indian cook. Capsicum (shimla mirch) and tomato are pantry staples. A swipe of green chutney inside the wrap instead of ketchup makes it distinctly desi.
